root.name: Add and Position Text Box Explaining 'Prostomium' on Slide 4
root.description: Evaluates whether the agent correctly adds a text box to Slide 4 with an explanation of 'prostomium' and positions it above the word 'prostomium' in the diagram, with no extraneous changes.

children.name: Presence of Added Text Box
children.description: Verifies that a text box was added to Slide 4.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    added = [s for s in ppt_diff.added_slides if s.slide_number == 4]
    if added:
        return "Slide 4 was added, not modified. Should not be the case.", 0.0
    # Get all shapes added to slide 4 (use added objects from pptx)
    from pptx import Presentation
    pres = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide = pres.slides[3]  # 0-indexed
    # Check for textboxes
    textbox_found = False
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.shape_type == 17:  # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.TEXT_BOX
            # Next, is it new?
            # Compare with original
            orig_pres = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
            orig_slide = orig_pres.slides[3]
            orig_texts = set(s.text.strip() for s in orig_slide.shapes if hasattr(s, 'text'))
            if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text.strip() and shape.text.strip() not in orig_texts:
                textbox_found = True
                break
    if textbox_found:
        return "A new text box was added to Slide 4.", 1.0
    else:
        return "No new text box added to Slide 4.", 0.0

children.name: Text Box Content is an Explanation, Not Just the Word
children.description: Ensures the new text box includes an explanation or definition of 'prostomium' rather than just the term itself.

children.name: Text Box Relative Position to Diagram Word
children.description: Ensures the new text box is above the occurrence of 'prostomium' in the diagram on Slide 4.

children.name: No Unrelated Slides or Content Modified
children.description: Ensures that only the required addition was made and no unrelated slides, shapes, or content were added, removed, or changed.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Only one shape added to slide 4 (the new textbox); no other changes allowed
    # Other diffs should be empty
    unrelated_changes = False
    # Check for slide adds/removes/other slide modifications
    if ppt_diff.added_slides or ppt_diff.removed_slides:
        return "Slides were added or removed.", 0.0
    # Any modified slides other than slide 4?
    for s1, s2 in ppt_diff.modified_slides:
        if s1.slide_number != 4:
            return f"Slide {s1.slide_number} was modified but shouldn't have been.", 0.0
    # Check for added/removed/modified objects on other slides
    # Check for more than 1 shape added to slide 4
    from pptx import Presentation
    pres = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide = pres.slides[3]
    orig_pres = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    orig_slide = orig_pres.slides[3]
    orig_texts = set(s.text.strip() for s in orig_slide.shapes if hasattr(s, 'text'))
    new_textboxes = [s for s in slide.shapes if s.shape_type == 17 and s.has_text_frame and s.text.strip() and s.text.strip() not in orig_texts]
    if len(new_textboxes) != 1:
        return f"Number of new text boxes on Slide 4 is {len(new_textboxes)} (should be 1).", 0.0
    # Check for added/removed/modified animations or transitions
    if ppt_diff.added_animations or ppt_diff.removed_animations or ppt_diff.modified_animations:
        return "Animations were changed.", 0.0
    if ppt_diff.added_transitions or ppt_diff.removed_transitions or ppt_diff.modified_transitions:
        return "Transitions were changed.", 0.0
    # Check for any added/removed/modified content on other slides
    # (We already checked slides above)
    return "No extraneous changes detected.", 1.0

metadata.task: Add a text box in Slide 4 explaining what 'prostomium' means and position it above the word  'prostomium' on the diagram
